name: Sour Cream Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease an 8-inch square pan and set aside.
2. In a heavy, medium saucepan, combine sour cream, milk, corn syrup, butter, and sugar. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar dissolves and the mixture comes to a boil.
3. Insert a candy thermometer into the mixture. Continue cooking until it reaches the soft-ball stage (238°F).
4. Remove from heat and pour the mixture into a large, heat-proof mixing bowl. Allow it to cool to lukewarm (115°F).
5. Stir in vanilla extract with an electric mixer until thickened.
6. Spread the mixture evenly into the prepared pan. Let it cool completely.
7. Once firm, cut the fudge into small squares using a knife. Place walnut halves on top of each piece.
8. Store in the refrigerator. Makes about 1 1/4 pounds.
